source_declaration.declaration_line: theorem choose_mul_succ_eq (n k : ℕ) : n.choose k * (n + 1) = (n + 1).choose k * (n + 1 - k) := by
source_declaration.name: Nat.choose_mul_succ_eq
source_declaration.source_snippet:
theorem choose_mul_succ_eq (n k : ℕ) : n.choose k * (n + 1) = (n + 1).choose k * (n + 1 - k) := by
  cases k with
  | zero => simp
  | succ k =>
    obtain hk | hk := le_or_gt (k + 1) (n + 1)
    · rw [choose_succ_succ, Nat.add_mul, succ_sub_succ, ← choose_succ_right_eq, ← succ_sub_succ,
        Nat.mul_sub_left_distrib, Nat.add_sub_cancel' (Nat.mul_le_mul_left _ hk)]
    · rw [choose_eq_zero_of_lt hk, choose_eq_zero_of_lt (n.lt_succ_self.trans hk), Nat.zero_mul,
        Nat.zero_mul]

hydrated_mathlib_context.why_needed: Used to relate binomial coefficients with different n.

fallback_mathlib_candidates.search_excerpt: /-- A lower bound on the multiplicity of `p` in `choose n k`. -/ theorem emultiplicity_le_emultiplicity_choose_add {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : ∀ n k : ℕ, emultiplicity p n ≤ emultiplicity p (choose n k) + emultiplicity p k | _, 0 => by simp | 0, _ + 1 => by simp | n + 1, k + 1 => by rw [← hp.emultiplicity_mul] refine emultiplicity_le_emultiplicity_of_dvd_right ?_ rw [← add_one_mul_choose_eq]
